source_text: Welcome back: Share your problem statement(s) with your table:
My pain point at work is that I spend time (doing a specific task).
If I could reduce or change this by (specific improvement),
I would gain (time, quality, or clarity impact).

records.title: Share your problem statement(s) with your table
bullets: My pain point at work is that I spend time (doing a specific task).
If I could reduce or change this by (specific improvement),
I would gain (time, quality, or clarity impact).

source_text: Explicitly describe what success looks like and how it will be evaluated

right.label: Goals
bullets: Narrow to one clear workflow (MVP)
Break work into steps
Define inputs, constraints, and stakeholders
Explicitly describe what success looks like and how it will be evaluated

rows.body: You are a [add an expert, such as a "social media expert"] and your objective is to [add goal, such as “create LinkedIn posts about events”]
“To complete these instructions, follow these steps”: Step 1, Step 2, Step 3 (etc)”
“When you are done, write your final response”: A summary paragraph of the work done; 3 bullet points, one per next step

records.caption: Use the + menu to add files, projects, and connectors — then turn on Research to investigate a question.

source_text: Build a Claude Project to answer user questions about material.
Start a new project and add some content you want people to know (e.g. your website’s FAQ)
Write a set of instructions telling the Project to respond to user questions only using the materials you gave it.

records.title: Build a Claude Project to answer user questions about material.
card: Start a new project and add some content you want people to know (e.g. your website's FAQ)
Write a set of instructions telling the Project to respond to user questions only using the materials you gave it.

source_text: “I want an AI that answers my and my team’s questions about our company materials.”
Prompt: Describe how it should interact with your team and your materials.
Files: Share the materials you want it to pull from.

records.title: Coach / Librarian
bullets: “I want an AI that answers my and my team's questions about our company materials.”
Prompt: Describe how it should interact with your team and your materials.
Files: Share the materials you want it to pull from.

source_text: Vanilla Claude Chat can write simple code for you!
Don’t think about it as “coding” - you are merely asking for a computer to do something annoying for you.
The mad lib to start with“Make me __________ that can run in my browser” “Make me an HTML file that…”
It’s very helpful to be very specific about the STEPS section of your prompt if your task is specific or complex.

records.title: Code(ish)!
bullets: Vanilla Claude Chat can write simple code for you!
Don't think about it as “coding” - you are merely asking for a computer to do something annoying for you.
The mad lib to start with: “Make me __________ that can run in my browser” / “Make me an HTML file that…”
It's very helpful to be very specific about the STEPS section of your prompt if your task is specific or complex.

source_text: Have Claude make you a new website
Ask Claude to “recreate the website at <URL>”
Optional: Give it any requests for style, features you want

records.title: Have Claude make you a new website
card: Ask Claude to “recreate the website at <URL>”
Optional: Give it any requests for style, features you want

records.caption: Ask Claude to recreate a site at a URL — it reads the front-end design for best practices, fetches details, and rebuilds the page.

source_text: Grab a piece of paper and draw the steps of how you make toast.
When you’re finished, share your steps with your team.
Notice what you had the same/different.

records.title: Grab a piece of paper and draw the steps of how you make toast.
card: When you're finished, share your steps with your team.
Notice what you had the same/different.

records.caption: Drawing how you make toast — the classic systems-thinking warm-up.
A workflow is a chain of nodes. Each box is a node; the arrows are the flow between them.
The gap is usually not the tool — it is the missing handoff between "this looks useful" and "this runs inside the business every week."

source_text: For any single step in a workflow, you can break it down into:
The result of the step and how you know it’s successful
The signal to start the step
The actions the actor does to complete the step
Info or data needed to complete the step (this is optional!)
The person or thing that completes the step

records.title: For any single step in a workflow, you can break it down into:

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: The signal to start the step

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The person or thing that completes the step

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: Info or data needed to complete the step (this is optional!)

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: The actions the actor does to complete the step

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The result of the step and how you know it's successful

source_text: TRY IT: ZOOM IN ON ONE STEP (NODE)
Apply the Steps to this Node: Getting Toast Out of the Toaster
The toast is out of the toaster!
You reach out your hand and pick up the toast
None needed, just hunger! (Maybe some jam)

records.eyebrow: TRY IT: ZOOM IN ON ONE STEP (NODE)
records.title: Apply the Steps to this Node: Getting Toast Out of the Toaster

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: You hear the toaster ding

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The person grabbing toast

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: None needed, just hunger! (Maybe some jam)

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: You reach out your hand and pick up the toast

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The toast is out of the toaster!

records.caption: The five components of a node: Trigger, Actor, Inputs (optional), Action, Success.
records.note: Merged slides 22 (generic definitions) and 23 (toast example applied); 23 is the fullest applied version. Generic defs: Trigger = the signal to start the step; Actor = the person or thing that completes the step; Inputs = info or data needed to complete the step (optional); Action = the actions the actor does to complete the step; Success = the result of the step and how you know it's successful.

source_text: “I WANT AI TO MATCH OUR GRADUATES TO THE BEST JOBS.”

records.title: “I want AI to match our graduates to the best jobs.”
records.subtitle: A real ask we'll map into a workflow.
records.note: Merged slides 24 and 25 (identical; 25 is a struck-through animation build). Used src 25 = last slide of group.

source_text: Problem: How to Match Our Graduates to the Best Job
Node 1: “I search five job sites for new jobs since my last search”
Node 2: “I save the jobs that match my students’ interests
Node 3: “I present the matches to each student. If they are interested we apply.

source_text: The node model maps to how you design your AI to run that step!
The output and how we know it did a good enough job
The steps you laid out in your prompt
What data/ context does it need? Role and goal
What starts your AI process (e.g. opening ChatGPT, a notification)

records.title: The node model maps to how you design your AI to run that step!

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: What starts your AI process (e.g. opening ChatGPT, a notification)

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The AI

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: What data/ context does it need? Role and goal

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: The steps you laid out in your prompt

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The output and how we know it did a good enough job

source_text: Add job links to a list with a description

records.title: The node model maps perfectly to how you design your AI to run that step!

left.label: Process Today
bullets: TRIGGER — You need more jobs
ACTOR — Human
INPUT — List of Sites
STEPS — Go to website; Input key words; Add job links to a list with a description
SUCCESS — List of jobs

right.label: Process with AI (your prompt sections)
bullets: TRIGGER — A calendar notification goes off
ACTOR — Claude Project
INPUT — Role, Goal, and Context
STEPS — Search the sites; Get new jobs (with context on what a good, new job is)
SUCCESS — Output: 5-10 jobs based on all the sites, the URLs

records.note: Merged slides 30 & 31 (near-identical node-anatomy table; 31 only relabels the 'Process with AI' column with prompt-section names Role/Goal/Context, Steps, Output). Combined the fullest content from both into one record at the last slide number (31).

source_text: We’ll check back in here at 12:00
If you finish early, you can try creating the prompt that would run this part of the workflow.

records.title: Map your workflow
card: Choose one specific workflow you want to improve based on your Mad Lib
Map the workflow as it happens today (draw it out!):
Identify one step that feels repetitive, slow, or unclear. Break down trigger, actor, inputs, action, success
records.question: (45 min) We’ll check back in here at 12:00. If you finish early, you can try creating the prompt that would run this part of the workflow.

source_text: AI performs all or most of the nodes with minimal or no human intervention, handling tasks from start to finish.
All nodes are performed manually by a human without any AI assistance.
Human performs some nodes with support from AI tools that handle some of the more repetitive or time consuming nodes.

records.title: Fully human → Fully automated

steps.label: Fully Human
steps.sub: All nodes are performed manually by a human without any AI assistance.

steps.label: AI-Assisted Human
steps.sub: Human performs some nodes with support from AI tools that handle some of the more repetitive or time consuming nodes.

steps.label: Fully Automated
steps.sub: AI performs all or most of the nodes with minimal or no human intervention, handling tasks from start to finish.

records.caption: The spectrum of AI involvement across your workflow nodes.
